title: eriogonum capillare
intro: Eriogonum capillare, also known as Needle Spine Buckwheat or Needle Spine Eriogonum, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico, characterized by its grayish-green foliage and small, white or pink flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Eriogonum capillare include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, making it a valuable natural remedy for various health conditions. Eriogonum capillare can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and it is often used topically to treat skin conditions, such as wounds, eczema, and acne.
